mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output that translates into higher corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns following month-over-month INDPRO acceleration do not exceed the unconditional baseline win rate of 65.5% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 0.25 percent, restrictive monetary policy elevates borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.25% over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in consumer prices alongside a stable or declining federal funds rate generate negative real interest rates that inflate nominal corporate earnings and drive positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 4.0 percent during late-cycle expansions indicates an overheating labor market that triggers expected monetary tightening and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are non-negative in more than 55% of observations when UNRATE is below 4.0% during late-cycle periods over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail and food services sales reflects robust consumer spending that directly lifts corporate top-line revenues, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS growth months fail to produce S&P 500 5-day forward returns exceeding the unconditional positive rate over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts coupled with a declining federal funds rate signal a credit-fueled construction expansion that lifts cyclical equity exposure, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases for 2 consecutive months while FEDFUNDS 3-month change is negative
failure_condition: The joint condition produces a t-statistic for positive returns below 1.5 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2 percent annualized reflects aggregate demand outperformance that reduces recession probability and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following GDPC1 growth above 2% are not statistically different from zero (p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding labor income that supports consumption and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with positive PAYEMS changes fail to generate S&P 500 60-day forward returns with an information coefficient above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
